title: WorldBlueprint
description: v2. The distilled, IMMUTABLE spine of a world: mostly pointers + a tiny spine, not an ontology (PRINCIPLES.md #2). Read-only at play time; the mutable, persistent instance lives in world_state.schema.json. v2 adds genre/voice/stats/dramatic_engine/stakes/space. Everything beyond id+title is optional so v1 blueprints still validate.

source_note.description: Short note on provenance. Engine never ships source text; references the user's own materials.

genre.description: Genre as DATA, not a code branch. Drives register, stat names, and typical conflicts.

conventions.description: Genre conventions in play, e.g. 'the house as antagonist'.

tone.description: v1 field, still accepted. Prefer `voice` in v2.

voice.description: Prose register the narration must honor each turn (the 'prose guidelines' lever).

exemplar_refs.description: RAG chunk ids; POINTERS, not copied text.

rules.description: The world's 'physics'/conventions AND hard constraints (e.g. epistemic lock: no knowledge beyond the epoch). The engine enforces these against drift.

lexicon.description: Signature vocabulary/coinages so output sounds in-world.

stats.description: The quality gauges the engine tracks (QBN). Two core gauges get genre-styled names; extra axes as needed.

dramatic_engine.description: Hidden conflict levers (Polti's 36). Genre-independent; never shown to the player.

situations.description: Polti situation names in play, e.g. 'Revolt', 'The Enigma'.

note.description: How the situations manifest in this world.

stakes.description: Consequence model + EMERGENT end conditions (not a branch tree).

space.description: Spatial model. SEEDS the map and sets how space works; the full map MATERIALIZES into world_state at play and persists there (stability). Not enumerated here (PRINCIPLES.md #2).

scale.description: Spatial scale, e.g. 'a single manor', 'a sprawling megacity', 'a star sector'.

traversal.description: How movement works / its costs, e.g. 'on foot via named exits', 'fast-travel between districts', 'jump drive between systems'.

regions.description: A few high-level regions as light seeds.

seeds.description: Light starting scaffolding. A FEW key places/factions/archetypes — the rest materializes at play.

opening.description: Initial player state for a new save.

when.description: A condition over stats/axes/flags that fires this ending, e.g. 'resilience.Sanity <= 0'.

exits.description: Seeded connections to other places (the map graph). More materialize at play.

direction.description: Optional direction label, e.g. 'north', 'up', 'through the iron door'.
